title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, write a function that determines if n is a power of two. An integer n is a power of two if there exists an integer x such that n == 2^x. For example, 1 (which is 2^0), 2 (which is 2^1), and 4 (which is 2^2) are all powers of two.

examples.explanation: 16 is 2^4, thus it is a power of two.
5 cannot be expressed as 2^x for any integer x, hence it is not a power of two.
0 is not a valid power of two since no integer x makes 2^x = 0.

hints: Consider how binary representation relates to powers of two.
Explore using bitwise operations to determine if n is a power of two.
Remember that only one bit should be set in the binary representation of a power of two.
Think about edge cases, especially when n is less than or equal to zero.
